body:
## Summary

Testing latest versions against the reference test document (#90). JS browser capture has major improvements but still has gaps. Rust browser capture is completely broken — times out instantly on any URL without launching Chrome.

**Test document:** https://docs.google.com/document/d/1f5zI2xOFpKa90v0GjamO_t7lqSdzMlaM/edit

## Rust v0.3.3: `--capture browser` times out instantly

The browser fails on **any URL**, not just Google Docs:

```
$ RUST_LOG=debug web-capture "https://example.com" --capture browser -f markdown --verbose
DEBUG web_capture: starting capture url=https://example.com format=markdown capture=browser
INFO  web_capture::browser: Rendering HTML for URL: https://example.com
Error: Browser error: Timed out waiting for headless Chrome to render https://example.com
```

```
$ RUST_LOG=debug web-capture "https://docs.google.com/document/d/<DOC_ID>/edit" --capture browser --archive --verbose
DEBUG web_capture: starting capture url=...edit capture=browser
DEBUG web_capture: selected Google Docs capture method method=BrowserModel
DEBUG web_capture::gdocs: capturing Google Doc editor model with a real browser
INFO  web_capture::browser: Rendering HTML for URL: ...edit
Error: Browser error: Timed out waiting for headless Chrome to render ...edit
```

The error is immediate (no wait) — it appears Chrome is not being found or launched. Chrome is available on this system at `/Applications/Google Chrome.app` and multiple Puppeteer Chrome versions are cached in `~/.cache/puppeteer/chrome/`.

Note: v0.3.2 browser capture worked (it extracted `DOCS_modelChunk` via HTTP fetch). v0.3.3 switched to a real browser launch but it fails to start.

## Rust v0.3.3: `--capture api` improvements

Good progress — two fixes from #92 are resolved:
- ` ` entities now decoded to regular spaces ✅
- Google redirect URLs now unwrapped ✅

### Remaining `--capture api` issues

| Feature | Issue |
|---------|-------|
| **Headings** | H1/H2 use underline style (`=====`/`------`) with empty `[]()` anchors prepended to each heading |
| **Bold/italic/strikethrough** | All inline formatting lost |
| **Blockquotes** | `>` prefix missing — plain paragraphs |
| **Nested lists** | Flattened — all items at same level, nesting lost |
| **Mixed lists** | Ordered/unordered nesting lost |
| **Link with bold text** | Split into 3 separate links: `[Link with ](url)[bold](url)[ text](url)` |

## JS v1.7.11: `--capture browser` improvements

Major progress since v1.7.9:
- Tables now multi-column ✅
- Ordered list numbering now sequential ✅
- Images now downloaded to `images/` directory (4/4) ✅
- Extra blank lines between list items removed ✅

### Remaining `--capture browser` issues

| Feature | Issue | Example |
|---------|-------|---------|
| **Tables have extra empty columns** | Each cell separated by an empty column | `\| A \|  \| B \|  \| C \|` (5 columns instead of 3) |
| **Nested list extra blank lines** | Blank lines between nested children | Extra `\n` between `Child item 1a` and `Child item 1b` |
| **Blockquote paragraph separation** | Missing `>` between paragraphs in multi-paragraph blockquotes | Second paragraph loses `>` prefix |
| **Nested bold+italic escaping** | Produces `*****` instead of proper nesting | `**Bold with *****italic*****` instead of `**Bold with *italic* and bold**` |
| **Trailing newline** | Missing final newline at end of file | `No newline at end of file` |

### JS `--capture api` remaining issues

Same as Rust API — formatting (bold/italic/strikethrough), headings (escaped numbers `1\.`), blockquotes, and nested lists are lost.

## Test results summary

| Metric | JS Browser | JS API | Rust API | Rust Browser |
|--------|-----------|--------|----------|-------------|
| Lines | 229 | 422 | 287 | **Timeout** |
| Bytes | 4,266 | 4,334 | 4,582 | **Timeout** |
| Images | 4/4 ✅ | 4/4 ✅ | 4/4 ✅ | **Timeout** |
| Headings | ✅ | ❌ escaped | ❌ underline+anchors | **Timeout** |
| Bold/italic | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | **Timeout** |
| Blockquotes | Partial | ❌ | ❌ | **Timeout** |
| Tables | Partial (extra cols) | ✅ | ✅ | **Timeout** |
| Links | ✅ | ❌ wrapped | ✅ unwrapped | **Timeout** |
| Lists | Partial | Partial | Flat | **Timeout** |

## Related issues

- #90 — Reference test document
- #92 — Previous remaining issues report (partially addressed)
- #80 — Rust `--capture browser` does not launch a browser (was fixed in v0.3.2, now broken again in v0.3.3)

## Applies to

- [x] Rust CLI (v0.3.3)
- [x] JavaScript CLI (v1.7.11)

comments.body:
We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration.

If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code.

title: v0.3.3 / v1.7.11: Rust browser capture times out instantly; JS browser remaining gaps
